body:
Bumps [eslint](https://github.com/eslint/eslint) from 9.39.4 to 10.2.0.
Release notes

Sourced from eslint's releases.

v10.2.0

Features

  • 586ec2f feat: Add meta.languages support to rules (#20571) (Copilot)
  • 14207de feat: add Temporal to no-obj-calls (#20675) (Pixel998)
  • bbb2c93 feat: add Temporal to ES2026 globals (#20672) (Pixel998)

Bug Fixes

  • 542cb3e fix: update first-party dependencies (#20714) (Francesco Trotta)

Documentation

  • a2af743 docs: add language to configuration objects (#20712) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 845f23f docs: Update README (GitHub Actions Bot)
  • 5fbcf59 docs: remove sourceType from ts playground link (#20477) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • 8702a47 docs: Update README (GitHub Actions Bot)
  • ddeaded docs: Update README (GitHub Actions Bot)
  • 2b44966 docs: add Major Releases section to Manage Releases (#20269) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • eab65c7 docs: update eslint versions in examples (#20664) (루밀LuMir)
  • 3e4a299 docs: update ESM Dependencies policies with note for own-usage packages (#20660) (Milos Djermanovic)

Chores

  • 8120e30 refactor: extract no unmodified loop condition (#20679) (kuldeep kumar)
  • 46e8469 chore: update dependency markdownlint-cli2 to ^0.22.0 (#20697) (renovate[bot])
  • 01ed3aa test: add unit tests for unicode utilities (#20622) (Manish chaudhary)
  • 811f493 ci: remove --legacy-peer-deps from types integration tests (#20667) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 6b86fcf chore: update dependency npm-run-all2 to v8 (#20663) (renovate[bot])
  • 632c4f8 chore: add prettier update commit to .git-blame-ignore-revs (#20662) (루밀LuMir)
  • b0b0f21 chore: update dependency eslint-plugin-regexp to ^3.1.0 (#20659) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 228a2dd chore: update dependency eslint-plugin-eslint-plugin to ^7.3.2 (#20661) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 3ab4d7e test: Add tests for eslintrc-style keys (#20645) (kuldeep kumar)

v10.1.0

Features

  • ff4382b feat: apply fix for no-var in TSModuleBlock (#20638) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • 0916995 feat: Implement api support for bulk-suppressions (#20565) (Blake Sager)

Bug Fixes

  • 2b8824e fix: Prevent no-var autofix when a variable is used before declaration (#20464) (Amaresh S M)
  • e58b4bf fix: update eslint (#20597) (renovate[bot])

Documentation

  • b7b57fe docs: use correct JSDoc link in require-jsdoc.md (#20641) (mkemna-clb)
  • 58e4cfc docs: add deprecation notice partial (#20639) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • 7143dbf docs: update v9 migration guide for @eslint/js usage (#20540) (fnx)
  • 035fc4f docs: note that globalReturn applies only with sourceType: "script" (#20630) (Milos Djermanovic)
  • e972c88 docs: merge ESLint option descriptions into type definitions (#20608) (Francesco Trotta)
  • 7f10d84 docs: Update README (GitHub Actions Bot)
  • aeed007 docs: open playground link in new tab (#20602) (Tanuj Kanti)
  • a0d1a37 docs: Add AI Usage Policy (#20510) (Nicholas C. Zakas)

Chores

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 000128c 10.2.0
  • 1988fad Build: changelog update for 10.2.0
  • 542cb3e fix: update first-party dependencies (#20714)
  • a2af743 docs: add language to configuration objects (#20712)
  • 845f23f docs: Update README
  • 5fbcf59 docs: remove sourceType from ts playground link (#20477)
  • 8702a47 docs: Update README
  • ddeaded docs: Update README

title: chore(deps): Bump actions/checkout from 4 to 6

body:
Bumps [actions/checkout](https://github.com/actions/checkout) from 4 to 6.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/checkout's releases.

v6.0.0

What's Changed

  • Update README to include Node.js 24 support details and requirements by @​salmanmkc in actions/checkout#2248
  • Persist creds to a separate file by @​ericsciple in actions/checkout#2286
  • v6-beta by @​ericsciple in actions/checkout#2298
  • update readme/changelog for v6 by @​ericsciple in actions/checkout#2311

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/checkout/compare/v5.0.0...v6.0.0

v6-beta

What's Changed

Updated persist-credentials to store the credentials under $RUNNER_TEMP instead of directly in the local git config.

This requires a minimum Actions Runner version of v2.329.0 to access the persisted credentials for Docker container action scenarios.

v5.0.1

What's Changed

  • Port v6 cleanup to v5 by @​ericsciple in actions/checkout#2301

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/checkout/compare/v5...v5.0.1

v5.0.0

What's Changed

  • Update actions checkout to use node 24 by @​salmanmkc in actions/checkout#2226
  • Prepare v5.0.0 release by @​salmanmkc in actions/checkout#2238

⚠️ Minimum Compatible Runner Version

v2.327.1
Release Notes

Make sure your runner is updated to this version or newer to use this release.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/checkout/compare/v4...v5.0.0

v4.3.1

What's Changed

  • Port v6 cleanup to v4 by @​ericsciple in actions/checkout#2305

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/checkout/compare/v4...v4.3.1

v4.3.0

What's Changed

title: Improve agentic readiness
body: ## Changes Addresses agentic readiness gaps identified by [microsoft/agentrc](https://github.com/microsoft/agentrc) assessment. ### Added - `.editorconfig` — consistent code formatting - `.github/CODEOWNERS` — security governance - `.github/dependabot.yml` — automated dependency updates - `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — AI agent instructions - `AGENTS.md` — agent behavior, branch protection rules, issue workflow, verification policy - `CONTRIBUTING.md` — contribution guidelines - `.env.example` — environment variable template - Formatter/linter configuration (ruff.toml, .prettierrc, etc.) - CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) Closes #49 ---

title: Active node neighborhood view — focus graph on current node's connections
body:
Parent: #54

When a node is focused, the expanded map filters to its N-hop neighborhood.

## Blocked by
None — works with current graph data

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement N-hop neighborhood extraction function in graph.ts (1-hop default, 2-hop toggle)
- [ ] Add neighborhood/full toggle button to expanded overlay
- [ ] Pass filtered graph to overlay network when neighborhood mode active
- [ ] Show node count indicator ('Showing 12 of 156 nodes')
- [ ] Re-center button centers on focused node in neighborhood mode
- [ ] Verify with Playwright: neighborhood shows only connected nodes

title: Cluster group collapse — collapse cluster into summary node
body:
Parent: #54 (Sense-making epic)

## Problem

Clusters with many nodes crowd the graph. You want to see that Code exists and connects to things, without seeing every file.

## Solution

Click cluster in legend → all nodes collapse into one summary node (name + count badge + aggregated external edges). Click again to expand.

title: Cluster group collapse — collapse cluster nodes into summary node
body:
Parent: #54

Click a cluster to collapse its nodes into a single summary node.

## Blocked by
- #55 (layer toggles — establishes the pattern for filtering nodes from the graph)

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement cluster collapse function: replace N nodes with 1 summary node + aggregate external edges
- [ ] Add collapse/expand toggle per cluster in the legend
- [ ] Summary node shows: cluster name, icon, node count badge
- [ ] Collapsed cluster preserves layout position
- [ ] External edges aggregated (deduplicated, highest weight kept)
- [ ] Verify with Playwright: collapsing a cluster reduces node count, expanding restores

title: Edge type visual styling — colors, dashes, arrows per edge type
body:
Parent: #54

Render edges differently per type in vis-network. Prerequisite: #50 (typed edges) ✅ done.

## Tasks

- [ ] Define edge color/dash/arrow map in createGraphNetwork.ts
- [ ] Map KBEdge.type to vis-network edge options (color, dashes, arrows)
- [ ] Edge width proportional to weight
- [ ] Active node highlighting: focused node's edges colored by type, others dimmed
- [ ] Verify with Playwright: different edge types visually distinct

title: Layer toggles in HUD — show/hide file, content, work, concept layers
body:
Parent: #54

Add layer toggles to the HUD to show/hide groups of node types.

## Blocked by
None — node type info already in KBNode.source.type

## Tasks

- [ ] Define layer definitions: Content (authored), Files (file/directory), Work (issue/pr/commit), Concepts (design cluster)
- [ ] Add layer toggle state to HUD component (persisted in localStorage)
- [ ] Filter nodes by active layers before passing to buildGraph
- [ ] Filter nodes in the sidebar/overlay graph rendering
- [ ] Add layer toggle UI to HUD legend area (checkboxes with counts)
- [ ] Re-layout graph when layers change
- [ ] Verify with Playwright: toggling layers hides/shows correct nodes

title: Epic: Sense-making — make the graph human-readable at any scale
body:
## Vision

The graph has outgrown what a single human can comprehend. Sense-making is the top priority.

## The Layer Model

1. **File layer** — raw file system (directories, source files, configs)
2. **Content layer** — authored documentation that presents files at a higher abstraction
3. **Work layer** — issues, PRs, commits representing activity
4. **Concept layer** — specs, designs, architectural decisions

## Sub-Issues (Now — vis-network improvements)

| Issue | Title | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| #51 | Clickable file path references | ✅ Done |
| #52 | Edge-type-aware related panel ranking | ✅ Done |
| #55 | Layer toggles in HUD | Open |
| #56 | Edge type visual styling (colors/dashes/arrows) | Open |
| #57 | Cluster group collapse | Open |

## Active node neighborhood view
Filed as part of #55 — when a node is focused, the graph filters to its N-hop neighborhood.

## Related Issues (Prior Work That Feeds This)

| Issue | Title | Relationship |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| #50 | Typed directional edges | ✅ Done — prerequisite for #56 (edge styling) and #55 (layer filtering) |
| #48 | Inline link extraction | ✅ Done — feeds richer edge data into the graph |
| #47 | Multi-layer node identity | Open — same entity across layers (file node = content node) |
| #44 | Graph views as projections | Open — the view system that layers and filtering plug into |
| #42 | Graph store (SQLite) + providers | Open — provider metadata enables layer identification |
| #41 | Node mapping (nodemap.yaml) | Open — defines how files become content-layer nodes |
| #40 | Graph Provider Architecture | Open — parent architecture for the provider/layer system |
| #39 | Rich graph views | Open — overlaps with this epic; visualization improvements |
| #10 | Constellation Graph (vis-network) | ✅ Done — the current renderer this epic improves |
| #11 | HUD | ✅ Done — where layer toggles and controls will live |
| #9 | Visual Identity System | ✅ Done — node rendering that edge styling builds on |

## Later (New Renderer)

| Issue | Title |
|-------|-------|
| #53 | Evaluate WebGL renderer (Sigma.js / react-force-graph) |

## Eventually

- 3D layered graph (different layers on different planes)
- Semantic zoom (zoom into a cluster to see its detail, zoom out to see summary)

title: Evaluate WebGL graph renderer to replace vis-network at scale
body:
## Problem

vis-network struggles with our current graph size (~150+ nodes, ~250+ edges). Layout stabilization is slow, rendering is jittery, and we're not yet at the scale the provider system will produce. The graph provider architecture will generate 1000+ nodes with 5000+ edges.

## Research Summary

| Library | Rendering | Max Nodes | Custom Shapes | React | Status |
|---------|-----------|-----------|---------------|-------|--------|
| **Sigma.js** | WebGL | 10k+ | Yes (plugins) | Wrappers | Active (v3, paired with Graphology) |
| **react-force-graph** | WebGL (Three.js) | 10k+ | Yes | Native React | Active |
| **Cosmos** | GPU (WebGL/WebGPU) | 10k+ | Limited | Unknown | Emerging |
| **G6 (AntV)** | Canvas | ~10k | Yes (high) | Via Graphin | Active |
| **Cytoscape.js** | Canvas | <10k | Yes | Manual | Active but canvas-bound |

## Top Candidates

### Sigma.js + Graphology
- Best 2D WebGL option for 10k+ nodes
- Graphology handles graph data structure, Sigma renders
- Custom node programs (WebGL shaders) for shapes
- Force-directed via ForceAtlas2 (same algorithm we use now)
- Active v3 with modern API

### react-force-graph
- Native React component, WebGL via Three.js
- 2D and 3D modes
- Best for React integration
- Force-directed only (d3-force)
- Comprehensive styling

### Cosmos
- Full GPU simulation AND rendering
- Fastest at extreme scale
- Limited styling (no custom node shapes yet)
- Newest, least proven

## What We Need from a Replacement

- [ ] Custom node shapes (rounded rects with SVG/icon inside)
- [ ] Typed edge styling (different colors, dash patterns, arrows per edge type)
- [ ] Click-to-navigate on nodes
- [ ] Zoom/pan with controls
- [ ] Emphasize/dim nodes (active node + neighbors highlighted)
- [ ] Force-directed layout that stabilizes and stops
- [ ] Handle 5k+ nodes at interactive FPS
- [ ] Reasonable bundle size

## Recommendation

Evaluate **Sigma.js + Graphology** first — it matches our existing ForceAtlas2 layout, has WebGL rendering, supports custom node programs, and the Graphology data structure aligns well with our typed edge model. If custom node rendering is too limited, fall back to **react-force-graph**.

## Migration Path

1. Prototype: render current graph data with Sigma.js in the overlay
2. Compare: side-by-side performance at current node count
3. If good: migrate sidebar graph and minimap
4. If not: try react-force-graph

title: Edge-type-aware related panel ranking
body:
## Problem

The Related panel in the HUD shows the top 8 neighbors sorted by degree (connection count). This pushes out file nodes and other low-degree nodes, even when they have strong typed edges to the current node.

For example, `content-pipeline` has a `derived_from` edge (weight 3.0) to its source file `file-content/content-pipeline.md` and a `references` edge (weight 2.0) to `file-src/engine/parser.ts` — but neither appears in the Related panel because authored nodes with more connections rank higher by raw degree.

## Expected

The Related panel should rank by **edge weight × relevance**, not just neighbor degree. A `derived_from` edge (weight 5.0) should rank higher than a `mentions` edge (weight 0.5), regardless of the target node's degree.

## Proposed Ranking

`score = edge_weight * (1 + target_degree * 0.1)`

This preserves the preference for well-connected nodes but lets strong edges (contains, derived_from, imports) compete with high-degree-but-weakly-connected nodes.

## Additional: Group by Edge Type

Consider grouping the Related panel by edge type:

\\\
Source File
  content-pipeline.md (derived from)

References
  src/engine/parser.ts
  src/api/github.ts

Related Architecture
  Graph Engine (frontmatter)
  Type System (frontmatter)
\\\

This gives the user a structured view of different relationship types instead of a flat list.

## Verified via Playwright

`content-pipeline` has 2 file edges in the data but 0 file nodes in the Related panel — all 8 slots taken by higher-degree authored nodes.

title: Clickable file path references in reading view
body:
## Problem

When content mentions `src/engine/parser.ts` in the body, the edge exists in the graph data (type: `references`, source: `inferred`), but the text renders as a plain `<code>` tag — not clickable.

The user sees code-formatted text but can't navigate to the file node.

## Expected

File path mentions that have corresponding `file-*` nodes in the graph should render as clickable links in the reading view, navigating to `#/node/file-src/engine/parser.ts`.

## Implementation

In `ReadingView` (or a markdown post-processing step), scan rendered HTML for `<code>` tags containing file paths (`src/`, `scripts/`, etc.). If a matching `file-{path}` node exists in the graph, wrap the code tag in an `<a>` linking to that node.

## Verified via Playwright

Edge exists in data:
`content-pipeline → file-src/engine/parser.ts` (type: references)
But renders as plain code text, not a navigable link.

title: Typed directional edges — rich link system with filtering and layout
body:
## Content Node

See `content/spec-typed-edges.md` for the full design.

## Summary

Upgrade the edge system from `{ to, description }` to typed, directional, weighted edges with source tracking. Enable runtime filtering by edge type.

## Edge types

contains, derived_from, imports, references, frontmatter, mentions, cross_references, modifies, closes, related — each with default weight, color, and line style.

## What this enables

- Filter edges by type at runtime (hide mentions, show only imports)
- Nodes disappear when all their edges are filtered out
- Layout influenced by edge strength (strong = tight clustering)
- Active node highlighting with type-specific styles
- Prerequisite for meaningful graph views

## Phases

1. Type existing edges in parser/graph engine
2. Render edge types in vis-network (color, dash, arrow)
3. Runtime filtering controls in HUD

title: Inline link extraction — connections derived from content, not frontmatter
body:
## Content Node

See `content/spec-inline-link-extraction.md` in the knowledge graph for the full design.

## Core Principle

**Inline links ARE the graph edges.** A markdown link `[Graph Engine](graph-engine)` in the body should automatically create an edge. No frontmatter duplication.

## Why This Matters

1. **Duplication** — same relationship stated in body + frontmatter
2. **Staleness** — remove inline link, frontmatter persists
3. **Lost precision** — frontmatter is document-level; inline links carry section location
4. **Split breakage** — 1-to-N splitting puts frontmatter connections on parent, but inline links stay with their section

## Link Types

- Markdown: `[text](target)`
- Issue refs: `#N`
- Code imports: `import { x } from './file'`
- File containment: directory → file
- Git: commit → file

## Impact

- Frontmatter `connections:` becomes optional/supplementary
- `links` command changes: 'missing ref' means 'mention without link' not 'missing frontmatter'
- Edges carry `location` (line, section) for correct 1-to-N splitting

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Markdown `[text](target)` links auto-create edges
- [ ] Issue `#N` refs auto-create edges (already partial)
- [ ] Edges carry source type: inline, frontmatter, or inferred
- [ ] Edges carry location (line number, section heading)
- [ ] 1-to-N splitting assigns inline edges to correct section nodes
- [ ] `links` command suggests converting mentions to inline links
- [ ] Frontmatter connections that duplicate inline links are flagged

title: Link assessment — graph health analysis CLI command
body:
## Content Node

See `content/spec-link-assessment.md` in the knowledge graph for the full design.

## Summary

Add `npx @anokye-labs/kbexplorer links` command that analyzes the graph and reports:

- **Orphan nodes**: zero connections
- **Broken references**: connections to non-existent nodes
- **Weak clusters**: no cross-cluster edges
- **Missing cross-references**: content mentions file paths/titles without edges
- **Coverage gaps**: source files with no content node
- **Stale connections**: references to nodes from old provider runs

Interactive `--fix` mode offers to add missing connections, create skeletons, remove broken refs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `links` command added to kbexplorer-cli
- [ ] All 6 analysis categories implemented
- [ ] Output is actionable (specific nodes/files/connections named)
- [ ] `--fix` mode offers interactive corrections

title: Graph Provider Architecture — provider-based graph system
body:
## Design Specs

Structured as a spec tree in `specs/providers/`:
- `00-overview.md` — current architecture analysis + evolution path
- `01-node-mapping.md` — how files become nodes (nodemap.yaml, projections)
- `02-graph-store.md` — SQLite cache + provider interface
- `03-views-and-expansion.md` — graph views + on-demand + external

## Evolution Path (Crawl → Walk → Run)

### Crawl: Node Mapping (#41)
Define how files become nodes. `nodemap.yaml` works with the existing parser — no provider system needed yet. Adds display modes (code, tree, table, diagram). This is the foundation for everything else.

### Walk: Graph Store + Providers (#42, #43)
SQLite cache + provider interface. File and git providers with resolution depth limits.

### Run: Views + Expansion + External (#44, #45)
Graph views as projections. On-demand node expansion. External provider plugins.

title: feat: Replace GitHub API with local data for self-exploration
body: refs #29 ## Summary Adds manifest-only local mode that eliminates all GitHub API calls at runtime for self-exploration. A pre-build script generates `src/generated/repo-manifest.json` containing everything the app needs. ## What's included **Pre-build manifest** (`scripts/generate-manifest.js`): - Walks local file system for tree structure - Reads README.md, content/*.md, config.yaml from disk - Fetches issues/PRs via `gh` CLI (best-effort, graceful degradation) - Fetches recent commits via `git log` - Host-aware for submodule mode **Local loader** (`src/engine/local-loader.ts`): - Imports manifest and produces same KBNode[]/KBConfig as API loaders - Reuses existing parseMarkdownFile, treeToNodes, issueToNode - Mode detection: `VITE_KB_LOCAL=true` or manifest presence **Integration**: - `useKnowledgeBase` hook routes to local or remote loaders - `resolveImageUrl` respects `BASE_URL` in local mode - Vite plugin auto-generates manifest on dev/build start - `prebuild` script added to package.json build pipeline ## Test results - **36 unit tests passing** (manifest script + parser exports) - **E2E verified with Playwright**: 116 nodes, 219 edges, 16 clusters loaded from local manifest with zero API calls - Issues, PRs, commits, authored content, README all render correctly ## How to test ```bash # Generate manifest and run dev server in local mode VITE_KB_LOCAL=true npm run dev # Or on Windows PowerShell: $env:VITE_KB_LOCAL = 'true'; npm run dev ``` ---

title: Unit and scenario tests for local data pipeline
body:
Parent: #29
Depends on: all other issues

Comprehensive test coverage using Vitest:

### Unit tests for generate-manifest.js
- FS walk produces correct tree structure
- Content files read and embedded correctly
- Config parsed correctly; handles missing config
- README read correctly; handles missing README
- Issues/PRs/commits: captures gh CLI output; handles missing gh; handles auth failure
- Submodule mode: detects host root; reads from host paths
- Filters dotfiles, node_modules, dist

### Unit tests for local-loader.ts
- Mode detection (env var, manifest presence)
- Config loading (from manifest, fallback to default)
- Authored content parsing (all md files, skip non-md, empty content)
- Repo content (tree nodes, issue nodes with cross-refs, PR nodes, commit nodes, README node)
- Empty issues/PRs/commits arrays handled

### Scenario tests (integration)
- Fresh repo with no content → tree + README only
- Repo with authored content → content + tree + README
- No gh CLI → tree + README, issues/PRs/commits empty
- Submodule → reads from host directory
- Full round-trip: manifest → local loader → KBGraph

body:

Depends on: manifest script

Add a Vite plugin to `vite.config.ts` that runs `generate-manifest.js` on `buildStart`. This ensures the manifest is always fresh when starting dev or building.

In `src/api/github.ts`, update `resolveImageUrl()` to:
- Local mode: return `import.meta.env.BASE_URL + path` (served by Vite)
- Remote mode: return `raw.githubusercontent.com` URL (existing behavior)

This ensures images in `content/` resolve correctly in both modes.

title: Update useKnowledgeBase hook with mode routing
body:
Parent: #29
Depends on: local loader

Update `src/hooks/useKnowledgeBase.ts` to:
1. Check `isLocalMode()`
2. If local: use `loadLocalConfig`, `loadLocalRepoContent`, `loadLocalAuthoredContent`
3. If remote: use existing API-based loaders (unchanged)

The hook's public interface (`LoadingState`) stays the same.

title: Local content loader (local-loader.ts)
body:
Parent: #29
Depends on: manifest script, exported parsers

Create `src/engine/local-loader.ts` providing:

- `isLocalMode(): boolean` — true if `VITE_KB_LOCAL=true` or manifest exists
- `loadLocalConfig(): KBConfig` — reads config from manifest
- `loadLocalAuthoredContent(): KBNode[]` — parses markdown from manifest
- `loadLocalRepoContent(): KBNode[]` — processes tree/README/issues/PRs/commits from manifest

Reuses exported parser functions. Imports `src/generated/repo-manifest.json`.

title: Export parser functions for reuse by local loader
body:
Parent: #29

Export these functions from `src/engine/parser.ts` so `local-loader.ts` can reuse them:
- `parseMarkdownFile(path, raw)`
- `treeToNodes(tree, repoName, excludePaths?)`
- `issueToNode(issue)`
- Any other internal functions needed by local loader

These are currently module-private. Make them public exports without changing their behavior.

title: Pre-build manifest script (generate-manifest.js)
body:
Parent: #29

Create `scripts/generate-manifest.js` that generates `src/generated/repo-manifest.json`.

## What it does
- Walks local file system → produces GHTreeItem-compatible tree (filters dotfiles/node_modules/dist)
- Reads `README.md` from disk
- Reads `content/*.md` files → embeds raw markdown keyed by path
- Reads `config.yaml` → parses and embeds
- Fetches issues via `gh issue list --json ... --state all --limit 200` (best-effort)
- Fetches PRs via `gh pr list --json ... --state all --limit 200` (best-effort)
- Fetches commits via `git log` or `gh api` (best-effort, last 50)
- Host-aware: detects submodule mode and reads from host repo root

## Requirements
- Zero external dependencies (Node built-ins only + `gh` CLI)
- Graceful degradation when `gh` not installed (empty arrays, console warning)
- Also create `src/generated/.gitignore` to exclude the manifest

title: Replace GitHub API with local data for self-exploration
body:
## Problem

kbexplorer fetches ALL content via GitHub API at runtime — authored markdown, file tree, README, issues, PRs, commits, and config.yaml. This causes rate limiting (60 req/hr unauthenticated) and is wasteful for self-exploration where content is on disk.

## Solution: Manifest-Only Local Mode

A pre-build script generates a single `src/generated/repo-manifest.json` containing:
- **Authored content**: markdown files from `content/` read from disk
- **Config**: `config.yaml` parsed from disk
- **File tree**: local file system walk
- **README**: read from disk
- **Issues, PRs, commits**: fetched via `gh` CLI at build time (best-effort)

The app imports this manifest in local mode — zero API calls at runtime. Remote mode (GitHub API) is preserved for deployed instances exploring other repos.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `npm run dev` loads with zero GitHub API calls in local mode
- [ ] Authored content hot-reloads are not needed (manifest regeneration on dev start is sufficient)
- [ ] Issues, PRs, commits appear when `gh` CLI is available
- [ ] Explorer still works when `gh` CLI is not available (tree + README + authored content only)
- [ ] Remote mode (`VITE_KB_LOCAL` unset, no manifest) works exactly as before
- [ ] Comprehensive unit and scenario tests pass

body: ## Changes - **OverviewView**: Replaced custom HTML/CSS with Fluent Card, Badge, CounterBadge, Title1, Subtitle2, makeStyles grid layout - **LoadingScreen**: Fluent Spinner + Body1 (removed custom spinner CSS/keyframes) - **ErrorScreen**: Fluent MessageBar + Button (removed custom error styles) - **NodeVisual/visuals.css**: Fluent shape tokens (borderRadiusMedium, borderRadiusCircular) and color tokens (colorNeutralBackground3, colorNeutralForeground2, colorNeutralStroke2, colorNeutralBackground1) - **overview.css**: Stripped all legacy classes — styles now live in makeStyles ---

title: fix: font sizer, minimap layout, and constellation overlay
body: Fixes four issues: 1. **Font sizer broken** — .kb-prose was hardcoding font-size: 1.05rem, ignoring the CSS variable 2. **Width sizer partly broken** — now properly consumed 3. **Minimap layout wrong** — was using naive circular layout, now uses force-directed positions from a temp vis-network 4. **Minimap click wrong** — was navigating to #/graph, now expands a constellation overlay (like Mukaase) Also improved full-page graph readability: more spacing, font stroke on labels. ---

title: feat: responsive layout + example issues for default experience
body: ## Summary Responsive CSS audit across all views plus 8 example GitHub issues to enrich the default repo-aware experience. ### Responsive Layout (Task 1) - **Breakpoints**: Desktop (>1024px), Tablet (768–1024px), Mobile (<768px) - **Overview**: Card grid 3→2→1 columns with consistent breakpoints - **Reading**: Connections sidebar on desktop, stacked below on tablet/mobile. Prose respects `--prose-max-width` variable. - **Graph**: Legend repositions to bottom-center on mobile - **HUD**: Three-panel on desktop, no minimap on tablet, collapsed 60px on mobile (center nav only) - **Cross-cutting**: `responsive.css` handles hero sizing and overflow prevention on mobile ### Example Content (Task 2) Created 8 issues (#9–#14, #16–#17) describing kbexplorer's own architecture: - Visual Identity System, Constellation Graph, HUD, GitHub API mode - Theme System, Phase 2 Copilot vision, Keyboard navigation, Authored mode - Issues cross-reference each other to create a rich graph with meaningful connections ---

title: Authored mode: markdown with YAML frontmatter
body:
## Overview

While the default experience uses GitHub API data (#12), kbexplorer also supports an **authored mode** where content comes from a collection of Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. This mode is designed for curated knowledge bases — design systems, architecture decision records, learning curricula, or team wikis.

### Frontmatter Schema

Each `.md` file declares its metadata in YAML frontmatter:

`yaml
---
title: Authentication Architecture
cluster: security
emoji: 🔐
sprite: /images/auth-icon.png
hero: /images/auth-hero.jpg
connections:
  - id: session-management
    type: references
  - id: oauth-flow
    type: depends-on
tags: [architecture, security, phase-1]
---
`

The engine parses these files using `gray-matter`, builds the graph from explicit `connections` arrays, and assigns visuals based on the mode selected (#9).

### Rendering

All prose is rendered using Fluent tokens for typography and colors. The default font is **Segoe UI** (via `tokens.fontFamilyBase`) for body text and headings. Code blocks use **Cascadia Code** (via `tokens.fontFamilyMonospace`). Colors, spacing, and radii all come from Fluent tokens through `makeStyles` — no custom CSS variables or hardcoded values.

### Clustering

In authored mode, clusters come from the `cluster` field rather than GitHub labels. This gives authors explicit control over how nodes group in the overview grid and the constellation graph (#10). The same cluster colors and visual indicators carry through the HUD (#11) and reading view connections sidebar.

### When to Use Which Mode

- **Repo-aware** (default): Zero-config exploration of any GitHub repo. Great for project onboarding. PRs are filtered out — only issues, README, and directory structure become nodes.
- **Authored**: Curated, designed knowledge experiences. Requires content creation but offers full control over structure, visuals, and narrative flow.

title: Keyboard navigation
body:
## Keyboard Navigation

### Active Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `t` | Cycle theme (dark → light → sepia) |
| `←` / `→` | Previous / next node in reading view |

### Removed Shortcuts
- `/` and `Escape` (overview page shortcuts) — removed because the overview page no longer exists
- `g` (graph page shortcut) — removed because the standalone graph page no longer exists

### Guard
- All shortcuts are suppressed when focus is inside an `input`, `textarea`, or `contenteditable` element

title: feat: Azure Static Web Apps deployment + comprehensive README

title: Phase 2: Embedded Copilot conversation module
body:
## Vision

Phase 2 introduces an embedded **GitHub Copilot conversation panel** directly inside kbexplorer. Users will be able to ask questions about the knowledge base — "What issues are related to authentication?", "Summarize the architecture decisions", "What's blocking the next release?" — and receive contextual answers grounded in the loaded graph data.

### How It Would Work

1. A collapsible chat panel slides in from the right side of the reading view, hosted inside a Fluent Dialog component for consistent overlay behavior
2. The conversation context includes the current node's content, its connections, and the full graph structure
3. Copilot generates responses using the knowledge graph as grounding — not generic answers, but answers specific to *this* repository's issues and structure
4. Responses can include clickable node references that navigate within kbexplorer

### Integration Points

- The HUD (#11) would gain a chat toggle Fluent Button in the right panel
- The graph view (#10) could support "find nodes about X" queries that highlight matching nodes
- The reading view would show inline suggestions for related content based on what you're reading
- The overview could offer a "summarize this cluster" action per cluster group
- All conversation UI would use Fluent components (Card, Button, Input, Body1, etc.) to match the existing design system

### Technical Approach

This would use the GitHub Copilot Extensions SDK to embed a Copilot-powered experience. The knowledge graph (#12) provides structured context that makes responses significantly more relevant than raw repository search. The Fluent theme system (#13) would style the chat panel automatically through FluentProvider context.

This is a Phase 2 feature — the current Phase 1 focuses on making the exploration experience excellent on its own (#9, #10, #11).

title: Theme System: Fluent dark/light
body:
## Theme System

### Three Themes
There are **three** themes, not two:

| Theme | Base | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| **Dark** | `webDarkTheme` | Fluent default dark palette |
| **Light** | `webLightTheme` | Fluent default light palette |
| **Sepia** | Custom via `createLightTheme` | Warm amber brand ramp with 16 shades from `#1C1308` to `#FCF7F0` |

### Toggle
- Three-way cycle in the HUD tools strip using moon / sun / book icons

### Implementation
- Single `FluentProvider` instance at the top level of the component tree
- `html` element gets a background fallback of `#292929` to cover scroll-overflow areas in dark mode

title: Content from GitHub API: repo-aware mode
body:
## Content from GitHub API

### Data Sources
- Fetches **issues**, **README**, and the **full file tree** from the GitHub API
- PRs and commits are **not** fetched — removed due to unreliable linking between PR nodes and the rest of the graph

### File Tree Processing
- Generates a **repo-root** node, **directory** nodes (2 levels deep), and individual **source file** nodes for `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.md`, `.json`, `.yaml`, and `.css` files
- Each file node is parented to its containing directory node

### README Handling
- README content is decoded with `TextDecoder` instead of `atob` to fix UTF-8 encoding issues with base64 content
- Content-based connections are created via keyword matching: a connection forms when 60% or more of a target node's title words appear in the README body
- `#N` issue references in the README also generate explicit connections

### Issue Processing
- Issues with 2 or more headings are split into a **parent** node plus individual **section** nodes

### Caching & Error Handling
- `CACHE_VERSION` constant enables cache versioning; stale data is auto-cleared on version bump
- An error screen is displayed when 0 nodes load (typically due to API rate limiting)

### Hub Node Selection
- The hub node (center of the graph) prefers the `readme` node; falls back to the highest-degree node

title: HUD: Mukaase-inspired persistent control bar
body:
## HUD

### Two Distinct Layouts
The HUD renders differently depending on dock position:

**Sidebar layout (left/right dock) — Okoto-inspired:**
- Top ~50%: live vis-network constellation graph
- Resizable split handle (row-resize cursor) between graph and connections list (range 20–80%)
- Connections list: cards showing icon + title + description + cluster color bar
- Compact tools strip at the bottom: theme toggle, dock selector, Aa font-size slider, Width slider

**Horizontal layout (top/bottom dock):**
- Minimap canvas
- Navigation row
- Related-nodes strip
- Tools panel

### Docking & Persistence
- Collapsible and dockable to 4 positions (top, bottom, left, right)
- Dock position and collapse state persisted in `localStorage`

### Sizing
- Sidebar width: **25vw** default, resizable from 15vw to 50vw via a drag handle
- Graph/connections split: **50%** default, resizable from 20% to 80% via the row-resize handle

### Constellation Overlay
- Full-screen overlay triggered by the MAP button
- Floating legend card showing cluster colors and counts
- Opens with a scale-out animation

title: Constellation Graph: vis-network integration
body:
## Constellation Graph

### Layout — No Separate Page
- There is no dedicated graph view page; the standalone route was removed entirely
- The constellation lives inside the **sidebar** (left/right dock) as a live vis-network instance
- A full-screen **overlay** is available via the MAP button, with a floating legend card and a scale-out open animation

### Sidebar Graph Behavior
- Sidebar graph uses smaller nodes (size range 28–44)
- Physics engine freezes after initial stabilization to keep the layout stable during interaction
- **Node emphasis:** the current node and its immediate neighbors render at full opacity; all other nodes fade to 30%

### Pan & Stabilization
- **Bounded panning:** a custom pointer-based pan handler clamps movement to the node bounding box, preventing the user from scrolling into empty space
- **Stabilization settings:** 150 iterations with `updateInterval: 150` — the graph renders once after settling rather than animating every intermediate frame

### Shared Infrastructure
- `createGraphNetwork()` factory in `src/engine/createGraphNetwork.ts` is shared by both the sidebar instance and the overlay instance

title: Visual Identity System: 4 modes × 7 surfaces
body:
## Visual Identity System

### Icon Rendering
- Fluent icons sourced from `@fluentui/react-icons`, colored with their cluster color (not monochrome)
- Icons are drawn as SVG data URIs inside canvas shapes, with paths defined in `ICON_PATHS` (`nodeRenderer.ts`)

### Custom Node Shapes
- vis-network nodes use custom shapes via `ctxRenderer`: circles, rounded squares, and rounded rectangles
- Opaque node backgrounds prevent edges from showing through the node fill
- Disconnected nodes display a red **!** badge to flag orphan state

### Component Architecture
- `NodeVisual` component accepts a `clusterColor` prop for consistent color theming
- The 7 cluster surfaces still apply for UI chrome, but graph nodes bypass `NodeVisual` and use the custom canvas renderer directly

title: feat: theme system, keyboard navigation, and HUD
body: ## Summary Three cross-cutting features for kbexplorer: ### 1. Theme System (\src/hooks/useTheme.ts\) - Dark/light/sepia switching with CSS variables - Persisted in localStorage (\kbe-theme\) - Body class applied: \ heme-light\, \ heme-sepia\ (dark is default/no class) ### 2. Keyboard Navigation (\src/hooks/useKeyboardNav.ts\) - \ \ → cycle theme - \/\ → overview, \g\ → graph, \Escape\ → back - \←/→\ → prev/next node in reading view - Ignores input when focused on form elements ### 3. HUD (\src/components/HUD.tsx\ + \src/styles/hud.css\) - Fixed 170px bottom bar with three panels - **Left**: minimap canvas with constellation dots - **Center**: navigation (MAP button, prev/next, current node info, related nodes strip) - **Right**: reading tools (theme radio buttons, font size slider, column width slider) - Hidden on graph view, collapses to 60px on mobile (<768px) - Font size and column width persisted in localStorage ### Files Changed - \src/hooks/useTheme.ts\ — new - \src/hooks/useKeyboardNav.ts\ — new - \src/components/HUD.tsx\ — new - \src/styles/hud.css\ — new - \src/index.css\ — added light/sepia theme variables - \src/App.tsx\ — integrated hooks and HUD component ---

title: feat: reading view with prose, connections, and navigation
body: ## Reading View Implements the detail panel shown when navigating to \#/node/{id}\. ### What's included - **ReadingView component** (\src/views/ReadingView.tsx\) - Hero/sprite/emoji header based on visual mode - Markdown prose rendered via \.kb-prose\ styles - Connected nodes sidebar (desktop) / section (mobile) - Prev/next navigation within same cluster - Not-found state with back link - **Reading styles** (\src/styles/reading.css\) - Full prose typography (headings, code, blockquotes, tables, lists, links, hr) - Connection cards with cluster pills - Responsive layout: sidebar at 1024px+, stacked below 768px - Prev/next footer navigation - **App.tsx** — Wired \/node/:id\ route to ReadingView via ReadingRoute wrapper ---

title: feat: constellation graph view with vis-network
body: ## Summary Full-viewport interactive graph visualization at \#/graph\ using vis-network. ### Changes - **GraphView component** (\src/views/GraphView.tsx\): vis-network constellation with forceAtlas2Based physics, node sizing by degree, cluster-colored nodes via \getVisNodeConfig\ - **Cluster legend**: bottom-left overlay with click-to-filter (dims non-matching nodes) - **Navigation**: node click → \#/node/{id}\, back button → \#/\ - **Tooltips**: node hover shows title + connection count, edge hover shows description - **Styling** (\src/styles/graph.css\): dark theme, fixed full-viewport canvas, glassmorphic overlays - **App.tsx**: wired GraphView into the \/graph\ route Closes #5 ---

title: feat: overview card grid + loading/error states
body: ## Changes - **OverviewView** (\src/views/OverviewView.tsx\): Card grid landing page grouped by cluster with NodeVisual, connection counts, cluster pills, and hover effects (border glow + lift). Responsive 3→2→1 column layout. - **LoadingScreen** (\src/components/LoadingScreen.tsx\): Centered spinner + text + 6 skeleton card placeholders with pulse animation. - **ErrorScreen** (\src/components/ErrorScreen.tsx\): Warning icon + error message + retry button + rate limit reset detection. - **App.tsx**: Replaced inline loading/error JSX and placeholder overview with new components. Added overview.css import. - **overview.css**: All styles for overview grid, loading screen, and error screen using CSS variables from index.css. ---

title: feat: core modules — GitHub client, parser, graph engine, visual system
body: Implements the foundational runtime modules for kbexplorer. ## Modules | Module | Path | Purpose | |--------|------|---------| | GitHub client | src/api/github.ts | Fetch files, trees, issues, PRs with caching + rate limits | | Content parser | src/engine/parser.ts | Authored (frontmatter+markdown) + repo-aware (issues/PRs/README) | | Graph engine | src/engine/graph.ts | Builds edges, computes related nodes (max 8, by degree) | | Visual system | src/components/NodeVisual.tsx | 7 surfaces × 4 modes (sprites/heroes/emoji/none) | | KB hook | src/hooks/useKnowledgeBase.ts | Ties API + parser + graph into one loading hook | | Styles | src/styles/visuals.css | Hero overlay, blur bg, circular crops, animations | ## Default experience App loads in repo-aware mode, fetches content from anokye-labs/kbexplorer via GitHub API, and renders a node list. Closes #3 ---

title: Core modules: GitHub client, content parser, graph engine, visual system, router
body: Implement core runtime modules: GitHub API client, content parser (authored + repo-aware), graph engine, visual system (7 surfaces x 4 modes), router, useKnowledgeBase hook. Closes when build passes and app renders nodes from the kbexplorer repo itself.

title: feat: scaffold Vite + React + TypeScript project
body: Scaffolds the kbexplorer repo with Vite + React 19 + TypeScript (strict mode). ## What - Vite + React 19 + TypeScript project scaffold - Core dependencies: react-router-dom, vis-network, vis-data, gray-matter, marked, yaml, @octokit/rest - Type definitions: KBNode, KBGraph, KBConfig, VisualMode, NodeSource - Default config for repo-aware mode (explores anokye-labs/kbexplorer itself) - Dark theme base CSS with design tokens (Instrument Serif / General Sans / JetBrains Mono) - Clean App shell with HashRouter (overview, graph, reading routes) - MIT license, README Closes #1 ---

title: Repository scaffold: Vite + React + TypeScript + Azure
body:
## Summary

Set up the kbexplorer repository with Vite + React + TypeScript scaffold and Azure deployment config.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Vite + React + TypeScript project initialized
- [ ] Package dependencies installed: react, react-router, vis-network, vis-data, gray-matter, marked, yaml, @octokit/rest
- [ ] Azure Static Web Apps deployment config
- [ ] README with project description
- [ ] MIT license
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode
